_comment: TheOrc Foundry recipe TEMPLATE — theorc-fabric v0. Follow-on #2: the most differentiated specialist (docs/THEORC_FOUNDRY.md).
phase: pre-F-1 (no frozen task universe, no baseline report)

job.name: theorc-fabric-qwen25-1.5b-lora-v0
job.description: Diagnose and repair Context Fabric ingestion/retrieval failures. Evidence source already exists: the frozen CF benchmark corpus and its 150 real questions (docs/CONTEXT_FABRIC_BENCHMARK_CORPUS.md) plus failure-injection results.

dataset._dataset_note: Task universe not frozen. Candidate shape: retrieval-failure case (query + retrieved evidence + expected source) → diagnosis/repair action. The CF-7 benchmark harness (docs/CONTEXT_FABRIC_BENCHMARK_MANIFEST.md for the run/capture recipe, docs/CONTEXT_FABRIC_GRADING_SPEC.md for how a case gets scored as a failure) is the capture point.

gates.blocked_reason: Per docs/THEORC_FOUNDRY.md follow-on order this track starts after theorc-dataset-judge. Baseline = current deterministic retrieval pipeline (Tier 1/1.5 anchor ranking) measured on the frozen un-marked corpus — beware NoKvSlot artifacts when reading old benchmark scores.

stop_rule: If deterministic retrieval fixes (anchor phrases, coverage-aware fill) keep closing the benchmark gap, keep shipping rules — a specialist must beat the rule-based pipeline on the frozen corpus to justify itself.
